content:
<system_instruction>
You are working inside Conductor, a Mac app that lets the user run many coding agents in parallel.
Your work should take place in the /Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/dublin-v3 directory (unless otherwise directed), which has been set up for you to work in.
Each workspace has a .context directory (gitignored) where you can save files to collaborate with other agents.
The target branch for this workspace is main. Use this for actions like creating new PRs, bisecting, etc., unless you're told otherwise.

If the user asks you to work on several unrelated tasks in parallel, you can suggest they start new workspaces.
If the user asks for help with Conductor, ask them to email [humans@conductor.build](mailto:humans@conductor.build) or
click on the comment icon in the bottom left to send feedback.
</system_instruction>


<system-instruction>
To do immediately, before using any skills or any other tools:

Rename the branch using `git branch -m`. Do not check the current branch name.

Only do this once per session. If the branch has already been renamed, leave it.

Choosing a branch name: use concrete, specific language, avoid abstract nouns, and be concise (<30 characters). Use the prefix "marc/" before the name.

</system-instruction>

We got the feedback that people are not comfortable within an organization, that everybody can look at the full session of other users. So we need some kind of authorization concept that only allows admins in an organization to look at all the session details of all users and otherwise only allows users to see their own sessions. So I can see my sessions. If I'm an admin, I can see all of the sessions in the org. But if I'm not an admin, I cannot see the sessions of my coworkers. This needs to be implemented in the backend for retrieving the full sessions as well as on the front end to not link to the session view. So we're linking from various places into the session detail view and in those places usually we need to kind of already remove the link or show that it can't be clicked in order to have a solid UX. And then maybe also look at endpoints that potentially expose more session details. Everything that's like in the analytics, like in the materialized aggregated one, is everything there fine; just the raw session table with full session content is sensitive.

We got the feedback that people are not comfortable within an organization, that everybody can look at the full session of other users. So we need some kind of authorization concept that only allows admins in an organization to look at all the session details of all users and otherwise only allows users to see their own sessions. So I can see my sessions. If I'm an admin, I can see all of the sessions in the org. But if I'm not an admin, I cannot see the sessions of my coworkers. This needs to be implemented in the backend for retrieving the full sessions as well as on the front end to not link to the session view. So we're linking from various places into the session detail view and in those places usually we need to kind of already remove the link or show that it can't be clicked in order to have a solid UX. And then maybe also look at endpoints that potentially expose more session details. Everything that's like in the analytics, like in the materialized aggregated one, is everything there fine; just the raw session table with full session content is sensitive.

content.thinking:
Let me understand the task:

1. Add authorization so that only admins can see all session details, while regular users can only see their own sessions
2. Backend: Filter session retrieval endpoints based on user role (admin vs regular)
3. Frontend: Remove/disable links to session detail views for sessions that don't belong to the current user (unless admin)
4. Analytics/aggregated data is fine - only raw session data with full content is sensitive

Let me start by renaming the branch and then exploring the codebase.

input.description: Rename branch for session access control feature

content.text: Let me explore the codebase to understand the current session-related endpoints and frontend.

input.prompt:
I need to understand the session access control architecture in this codebase. Please find:

1. All API endpoints/routes that return raw session data (not aggregated analytics) - especially session detail endpoints
2. The current auth/authorization model - how users and organizations work, any existing role/admin concept
3. Frontend pages/components that link to session detail views
4. The router setup in apps/api - how routes are protected
5. Any existing user role or permission concept in the database schema (Postgres or ClickHouse)

Be very thorough - check apps/api/src/, apps/web/src/, packages/api-routes/, packages/sql-schema/, packages/ch-schema/. Look for things like "session detail", "getSession", "session_id", role, admin, permission.

content.text:
Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me compile my findings.

## Session Access Control Architecture Summary

Based on my thorough analysis of the Rudel codebase, here's the complete picture of how session access control is implemented:

### 1. API Endpoints for Session Data (Raw Sessions, Not Aggregated)

**Session Detail Endpoint (returns full raw session data with conversation content):**
- **Route**: `POST /rpc/analytics/sessions/detail`
- **Handler**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/dublin-v3/apps/api/src/handlers/analytics/sessions.ts` (lines 63-74)
- **Service**: `getSessionDetail()` in `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/dublin-v3/apps/api/src/services/session-analytics.service.ts` (lines 676-727)
- **Contract**: `SessionDetailInputSchema` (input: `sessionId`) and `SessionDetailSchema` (output) in `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/dublin-v3/packages/api-routes/src/schemas/analytics.ts` (lines 360-386)
- **Returns**: Full session data including:
  - `content` (raw conversation transcript)
  - `subagents` (map of subagent data)
  - `skills` and `slash_commands` (arrays)
  - Git metadata (`git_branch`, `git_sha`)
  - Token counts and success scores
  - Full session metadata and analytics

**Session List Endpoint (returns paginated analytics summaries, not full content):**
- **Route**: `POST /rpc/analytics/sessions/list`
- **Handler**: `getSessionAnalytics()` in `session-analytics.service.ts` (lines 70-201)
- **Returns**: Array of `SessionAnalytics` objects with metadata but NOT the full `content` field

### 2. Current Authorization Model

**Authentication Layer:**
- All analytics endpoints use `authMiddleware` or `orgMiddleware` (not public)
- Auth via `better-auth` with support for email/password, OAuth (GitHub/Google), and API keys
- Session tokens tracked in Postgres `session` table

**Organization-Based Access Control:**
- **Organization Model**: Each user has a personal workspace (org_id = user_id) and can join multiple organizations
- **Membership**: Tracked in Postgres `member` table with two columns:
  - `organizationId` (Foreign key to organization)
  - `userId` (Foreign key to user)
  - `role` (String: "owner", "admin", or "member")
  
**orgMiddleware Flow** (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/dublin-v3/apps/api/src/middleware.ts`, lines 29-70):
1. Checks user is authenticated (`UNAUTHORIZED` if not)
2. Gets `organizationId` from session's `activeOrganizationId` or defaults to `context.user.id` (personal workspace)
3. **For non-personal orgs**: Verifies user is a member by checking `member` table
4. **No member check needed** if `organizationId === context.user.id` (personal workspace)
5. Passes `context.organizationId` to handler

**Key Finding - Organization-Level Access Control:**
- ✅ Session detail access is **gated at the organization level**
- ✅ Users can only access sessions belonging to their active organization
- ✅ Query filters by `organization_id` in ClickHouse table
- ❌ **NO per-session role checks** - once you're a member of an org, you can access all sessions in that org
- ❌ **NO per-user role enforcement** in session access - `member.role` is stored but NOT checked in analytics endpoints
- The `member.role` field supports "owner" and "admin" (see `deleteOrganization` handler checking `role = "owner"`), but analytics endpoints don't differentiate by role

### 3. Role/Admin Concept

**Member Roles Defined:**
- `owner` - Can delete organization, manage members (enforced in `deleteOrganization` handler)
- `admin` - Same permissions as owner in frontend (`canEdit = role === "owner" || role === "admin"`)
- `member` - Basic organization member

**Role Storage:**
- Postgres `member.role` column (line 111 in `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/dublin-v3/packages/sql-schema/src/auth-schema.ts`)
- Default: "member"
- Better Auth organization plugin sets creator as "owner"

**Where Roles Are Actually Used:**
- ✅ Organization deletion check (requires `role = "owner"`)
- ✅ Frontend Organization page (shows `canEdit` logic for owner/admin)
- ✅ Invitation system (can specify role when inviting)
- ❌ **Analytics/session endpoints don't check roles** - only check organization membership

### 4. ClickHouse Schema (Session Storage)

**Tables:**
- `rudel.claude_sessions` - Source table (base schema)
- `rudel.session_analytics` - Materialized view with computed analytics

**Organization Partitioning:**
- `order_by: ["organization_id", "session_date", "session_id"]` (enables efficient org-level queries)
- `partitionBy: toYYYYMM(toDate(session_date))` (monthly partitioning for performance)
- All queries filter by `organization_id = {orgId:String}`

**Key Columns:**
- `organization_id` (String)
- `session_id` (String)
- `user_id` (String)
- `content` (String - full transcript)
- `session_date` (DateTime64)

### 5. Frontend Navigation to Session Details

**Session Detail Page:**
- **File**: `/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/dublin-v3/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/SessionDetailPage.tsx`
- **Route**: `/dashboard/sessions/:sessionId` (defined in `App.tsx`)
- **Data Fetch**: Uses `orpc.analytics.sessions.detail.queryOptions()` (React Query)
- **No client-side role checks** - assumes authorization is handled server-side

**Entry Points (where users navigate to session details):**
1. **Sessions List Page** (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/dublin-v3/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/SessionsListPage.tsx`, line 457):
   - Table click handler: `navigate(/dashboard/sessions/${row.session_id})`

2. **Developer Detail Page** (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/dublin-v3/apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/DeveloperDetailPage.tsx`, line 610):
   - Sessions table click: `navigate(/dashboard/sessions/${row.session_id})`

3. **Learnings Timeline** (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/dublin-v3/apps/web/src/components/learnings/TimelineItem.tsx`, line 72):
   - Learning entry click: `sessionLink = /dashboard/sessions/${learning.session_id}`

### 6. API Routes Definition

**Contract Structure** (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/dublin-v3/packages/api-routes/src/index.ts`):
```typescript
analytics: {
  sessions: {
    list: SessionListInputSchema → SessionAnalyticsSchema[]
    detail: SessionDetailInputSchema → SessionDetailSchema
    summary: DaysInputSchema → SessionAnalyticsSummarySchema
    summaryComparison: DaysInputSchema → SessionAnalyticsSummaryComparisonSchema
    dimensionAnalysis: DimensionAnalysisInputSchema → DimensionAnalysisDataPointSchema[]
  }
}
```

All analytics routes use `orgMiddleware` (line 2 in `sessions.ts`), ensuring organization-level access control.

### 7. Database Schema Relationships

**Postgres Auth Schema** (`/Users/marc/conductor/workspaces/rudel/dublin-v3/packages/sql-schema/src/auth-schema.ts`):
- `user` - User accounts
- `organization` - Orgs/workspaces
- `member` - Join table (user → org with role)
- `invitation` - Pending org invitations
- `apikey` - API keys for CLI (has `permissions` field as JSON text, used for ingest control)

**ClickHouse Schema:**
- `rudel.claude_sessions` - Raw session data (base table)
- `rudel.session_analytics` - Analytics computed view
- All filtered by `organization_id` at query time

### 8. Summary of Access Control Flow

**For session detail access:**
1. User makes authenticated request to `/rpc/analytics/sessions/detail`
2. `orgMiddleware` verifies:
   - User is authenticated ✓
   - User is member of the active organization ✓ (or is personal workspace owner)
3. Handler calls `getSessionDetail(context.organizationId, sessionId)`
4. ClickHouse query filters: `WHERE session_id = {sessionId} AND organization_id = {orgId}`
5. Returns full session data (or NOT_FOUND if session not in org)

**No additional checks:**
- ❌ User's role within organization
- ❌ Whether session belongs to them or another team member
- ❌ Per-session permissions/sharing
- ❌ Admin override capabilities

This is an **organization-based multi-tenancy model** where all org members have equal access to all session data in their organization.

**Backend changes:**
1. In `getSessionDetail` service - add `userId` parameter, check if user is admin/owner or if session belongs to them
2. In session detail handler - pass user ID to service
3. Maybe also filter the sessions list to only show own sessions for non-admins (or at least not expose content)

**Frontend changes:**
1. Need to know user's role in the current org
2. In SessionsListPage - conditionally make session rows clickable
3. In DeveloperDetailPage - conditionally make session rows clickable
4. In TimelineItem - conditionally make learning entries clickable


5. The session detail page needs to handle unauthorized access gracefully

   666→
   667→	return results.map((row) => ({
   668→		dimension_value: String(row.dimension_value),
   669→		metric_value: Number(row.metric_value),
   670→	}));
   671→}
   672→
   673→/**
   674→ * Get detailed session information including conversation content
   675→ */
   676→export async function getSessionDetail(
   677→	orgId: string,
   678→	sessionId: string,
   679→): Promise<SessionDetail | null> {
   680→	const query = `
   681→    SELECT
   682→      session_id,
   683→      user_id,
   684→      formatDateTime(sa.session_date, '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%i:%SZ') as session_date,
   685→      formatDateTime(sa.last_interaction_date, '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%i:%SZ') as last_interaction_date,
   686→      project_path,
   687→      if(git_remote != '', git_remote, if(package_name != '', package_name, project_path)) as repository,
   688→      content,
   689→      subagents,
   690→      skills,
   691→      slash_commands,
   692→      git_branch,
   693→      git_sha,
   694→      total_tokens,
   695→      input_tokens,
   696→      output_tokens,
   697→      success_score,
   698→      actual_duration_min as duration_min,
   699→      total_interactions,
   700→      session_archetype,
   701→      model_used
   702→    FROM rudel.session_analytics sa
   703→    WHERE session_id = {sessionId:String}
   704→      AND organization_id = {orgId:String}
   705→    ORDER BY ingested_at DESC
   706→    LIMIT 1
   707→  `;
   708→
   709→	const results = await queryClickhouse<SessionDetail>({
   710→		query,
   711→		query_params: {
   712→			orgId,
   713→			sessionId,
   714→		},
   715→	});
   716→
   717→	const [row] = results;
   718→	if (!row) {
   719→		return null;
   720→	}
   721→	return {
   722→		...row,
   723→		repository: row.repository || null,
   724→		git_branch: row.git_branch || null,
   725→		git_sha: row.git_sha || null,
   726→	};
   727→}
   728→

   346→	dimension: z.enum(VALID_DIMENSIONS),
   347→	metric: z.enum(VALID_METRICS),
   348→	splitBy: z.enum(VALID_DIMENSIONS).optional(),
   349→	limit: z.number().int().positive().default(20),

   356→	metric_value: z.number().optional(),
   357→	split_values: z.record(z.string(), z.number()).optional(),
   358→});
   359→
   360→export const SessionDetailSchema = z.object({
   361→	session_id: z.string(),
   362→	user_id: z.string(),
   363→	session_date: z.string(),
   364→	last_interaction_date: z.string(),
   365→	project_path: z.string(),
   366→	repository: z.string().nullable(),
   367→	content: z.string(),
   368→	subagents: z.record(z.string(), z.string()),
   369→	skills: z.array(z.string()),
   370→	slash_commands: z.array(z.string()),
   371→	git_branch: z.string().nullable(),
   372→	git_sha: z.string().nullable(),
   373→	total_tokens: z.number(),

	dimension: z.enum(VALID_DIMENSIONS),
	metric: z.enum(VALID_METRICS),
	splitBy: z.enum(VALID_DIMENSIONS).optional(),

});

});

});

Good - links to session detail are only in the 3 files I already found. Now I have enough context to implement this. Let me plan the approach:

**Plan:**

1. **Backend - `orgMiddleware`**: Fetch the user's role and pass it in context (along with `userId`). For personal workspaces (`orgId === userId`), treat as "owner".

2. **Backend - session detail handler**: If user is not admin/owner, add a `user_id` filter to the ClickHouse query so they can only see their own sessions.

3. **Frontend - `OrganizationContext`**: Expose the current user's role in the org so components can check it.

4. **Frontend - session links**: Conditionally enable/disable session detail links based on whether the session belongs to the current user OR the user is admin/owner.

Let me start implementing.

content.text:
All 11 tasks pass. Here's a summary of all changes:

## Changes Made

### Backend (authorization enforcement)

**`apps/api/src/middleware.ts`** - `orgMiddleware` now fetches the user's role from the `member` table and passes `userRole` and `isOrgAdmin` in context. Personal workspaces default to "owner".

**`apps/api/src/handlers/analytics/sessions.ts`** - The `detail` handler now checks: if the user is not an admin/owner, they can only view sessions where `user_id` matches their own user ID. Otherwise returns `FORBIDDEN`.

### Frontend (UX for non-admins)

**`apps/web/src/contexts/OrganizationContext.tsx`** - Added `isOrgAdmin` boolean to the context, derived from `authClient.useActiveMember()`. Exposes whether the current user is an owner/admin.

**`apps/web/src/hooks/useCanViewSession.ts`** (new) - Helper hook that returns a function `(sessionUserId) => boolean`. Admins can view all sessions; regular members can only view their own.

**`apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/SessionsListPage.tsx`** - Row click only navigates to session detail if the user can view that session.

**`apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/DeveloperDetailPage.tsx`** - Session table row click is disabled entirely when viewing another developer's sessions (non-admin).

**`apps/web/src/components/learnings/TimelineItem.tsx`** - "View Session" link is hidden for sessions belonging to other users (non-admin).

**`apps/web/src/pages/dashboard/SessionDetailPage.tsx`** - Shows "Access Denied" message if the backend returns FORBIDDEN (direct URL access).

### What's not affected

All aggregated analytics (summaries, dimension analysis, ROI, learnings content, developer stats) remain fully visible to all org members - only raw session transcripts with full conversation content are restricted.
